seoTitle: How to Pin an Excel File So It Stays on Top (2026)
metaDescription: Pin frequently used Excel workbooks to the top of your recent files list, plus how to add files to Favorites and unpin them when you're done.
intro: When you're juggling several trackers and templates, digging through your recent files list every time gets old fast. Excel lets you pin the workbooks you use most so they stay anchored at the top, and this guide shows the pushpin, right-click, and Favorites methods for locking them in place.

steps.heading: Open the File backstage screen
steps.body: Click the File tab in the top-left corner of Excel to reach the backstage view, which lists your recently opened workbooks.

steps.heading: Find the pushpin icon
steps.body: Hover over the name of the workbook you want to lock in place until a faded pushpin icon appears on the right edge of its row.

steps.heading: Click to pin the file
steps.body: Click the pushpin once to solidify it, which anchors the file permanently at the top of your list instead of letting it roll off with new files.

steps.heading: Check the dedicated Pinned tab
steps.body: Look for a separate Pinned category tab above your file history to confirm the workbook stays accessible even as you open other files.

steps.heading: Use right-click as an alternative
steps.body: Right-click any file name in the list and choose Pin to list, or select Add to Favorites to sync it across your Microsoft 365 profile.

faq.question: Does pinning a file move or copy it?
faq.answer: No, pinning only creates a shortcut in your recent files view; the original file stays exactly where it's saved.

faq.question: How do I unpin a file later?
faq.answer: Click the solid pushpin icon again on the pinned file's row to release it back into the regular recent files list.

faq.question: What's the difference between pinning and adding to Favorites?
faq.answer: Pinning keeps a file at the top of your local recent list, while Favorites can sync across devices when you're signed into a Microsoft 365 profile.

faq.question: Can I pin both local and OneDrive files?
faq.answer: Yes, the pinning feature works for offline local files as well as cloud-hosted OneDrive workbooks.
